_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Broward delegation HD-98..102 (Rosenwald, Campbell, LaMarca, Cassel, Gottlieb). State tier. Roll calls verified against official FL Legislature vote PDFs. Catches: LaMarca + Gottlieb term-limited (candidacy fix); Cassel D->R party-switcher (prior 97/100 was party-inferred, corrected down on hard evidence); LaMarca R-deviations (NAY 6wk ban, NAY Don't Say Gay); Gottlieb pro-2A sponsorship deviation.

profile.education: BA sociology/economics, Salisbury University; MA sociology, Syracuse University; MSW & PhD social work, University of Maryland Baltimore
profile.background: Professor of social work and director of doctoral studies, Barry University; FL licensed clinical social worker. Former Oakland Park Mayor (2023-24), Vice Mayor, City Commissioner (2020-24). Openly gay legislator.

mitch-rosenwald@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-98 (Broward), Democrat, sworn in Nov 2024. Uniformly progressive record; no conservative cross-party deviations found. HARD: voted NAY on HB 759 (2025) to restore the firearm-purchase age (i.e., voted to keep the gun-age restriction); co-introducer of HB 65 (2025) gun-control package (universal background checks, safe-storage, ghost-gun limits); endorsed by LGBTQ Victory Fund and Equality Florida (openly gay). Mild cross-current: endorsed by Broward Fraternal Order of Police. No abortion roll call or voucher vote located (freshman, sponsors little); Planned Parenthood PAC page inaccessible so PP funding unverified -> null, not assumed.

0.note: Openly gay; backed by LGBTQ Victory Fund and Equality Florida -- does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman.

1.note: LGBTQ Victory Fund-endorsed, openly gay -- supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Equality Florida-endorsed ally on LGBTQ/transgender bills -- does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Victory Fund + Equality Florida-backed; supports LGBTQ identity in public policy and schools.

1.note: Co-introducer of HB 65 (2025) gun-control package (universal background checks, mandatory safe storage, ghost-gun limits) and voted to keep the post-Parkland gun-age restriction (NAY on HB 759); Moms Demand Action-endorsed.

profile.education: BA, Jacksonville University (2010); MSW, Barry University (2017)
profile.background: Mental-health therapist / Licensed Clinical Social Worker. In FL House since a Feb 2022 special election.

daryl-campbell@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-99 (Fort Lauderdale/Broward), Democrat. Consistent progressive; conservative Freedom Index lifetime 13%. All FALSEs verified against official FL Legislature roll-call PDFs: NAY on SB 300 (2023 6-week abortion ban), NAY on HB 543 (2023 permitless carry), NAY on HB 1 (2023 universal vouchers), NAY on SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement/E-Verify/anti-sanctuary). Planned Parenthood (FL)-endorsed. No genuine cross-party conservative deviation found.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week abortion ban -- pro-choice position; does not affirm personhood from conception in law.

1.note: Voted against even a 6-week restriction -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers; Freedom Index notes NO on expanding tax-credit scholarships.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023), which restricts sanctuary policies and strengthens immigration enforcement.

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

profile.education: AA, Broward College (also coursework at Florida Atlantic University and Boston University)
profile.background: Owner, LaMarca Construction Corp. Former Broward County Commissioner (2010-2018, the lone Republican on the board) and Lighthouse Point City Commissioner. In FL House since 2018.

chip-lamarca@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-100 (coastal Broward), Republican. TERM-LIMITED -- cannot seek reelection in 2026; HD-100 is an open seat (won his 4th/final term in 2024). Comparative moderate with documented R-deviations AND conservative votes. DEVIATIONS (HARD): voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week abortion ban -- joined Democrats; voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022) Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay'); did NOT vote on HB 1 (2023) universal vouchers (official roll call lists him Not Voting). CONSERVATIVE (HARD): supported HB 931 (2024) school chaplains (all Republicans); supported SB 1580 (2023) medical conscience/right-to-refuse-care; co-sponsor of SB 1718 (2023) immigration enforcement (incl. E-Verify mandate). Freedom Index lifetime ~57%.

1.note: DEVIATION: voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week ban, joining Democrats -- does not advocate abortion abolition or even this restriction.

4.note: DEVIATION: voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022) Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay') -- did not support restricting LGBTQ instruction in schools.

1.note: Voted for SB 1580 (2023) protecting medical providers' conscience/right to refuse care -- conscience exemption.

3.note: Supported HB 931 (2024) authorizing volunteer school chaplains (passed with all Republicans incl. LaMarca).

2.note: Co-sponsor of SB 1718 (2023), which restricts sanctuary policies and strengthens immigration enforcement.

3.note: Co-sponsor of SB 1718, which mandates E-Verify for larger employers.

profile.education: BS political science, Florida State University (2003); JD, Nova Southeastern University (Shepard Broad College of Law)
profile.background: Attorney; founder, Cassel & Cassel (2017); former Assistant State Attorney (2006-2009). Elected to FL House as a Democrat (2022, 2024); switched to the Republican Party on Dec 27, 2024.

hillary-cassel@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-101 (Hollywood/Dania Beach, Broward). PARTY SWITCHER: elected as a Democrat, switched D->R on Dec 27, 2024, citing the Democratic Party's stance on Israel/antisemitism -- NOT the social issues in this rubric. The prior 97/100 score was party-inferred and is corrected here on documented evidence. There is NO evidence of conservative positions on life, marriage, or guns; the available HARD evidence is contrary: endorsed as a Democrat by Ruth's List Florida (pro-abortion-rights PAC) and by Equality Florida (to which she 'promised to defend equality'); post-switch (2025) she voted NAY on HB 759 -- siding with Democrats to keep the gun-purchase-age restriction. Gov. DeSantis publicly called her 'very, very liberal' and doubted the switch. Confirmed running for HD-101 reelection in 2026 as a Republican. Score reflects documented positions, not party label.

0.note: Endorsed (and accepted backing) by Ruth's List Florida, a pro-abortion-rights PAC -- pro-choice; does not affirm personhood from conception in law.

1.note: Ruth's List-backed pro-choice candidate -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Accepted endorsement from Ruth's List Florida (pro-abortion-rights PAC).

1.note: Sought and accepted Equality Florida's endorsement and 'promised that she would defend equality' -- supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Equality Florida-endorsed (LGBTQ/transgender-rights group); does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Promised Equality Florida she would 'defend equality' -- supports LGBTQ identity in public policy/schools.

1.note: Post-switch (2025) voted NAY on HB 759 -- one of few Republicans siding with Democrats to KEEP the gun-purchase-age restriction (gun-control position).

profile.education: BA political science, George Washington University (1990); JD, Nova Southeastern University (1993)
profile.background: Criminal defense attorney (~25 years; Michael A. Gottlieb, P.A., Broward). FL House since 2018 (HD-98 2018-2022, HD-102 2022-present). Democratic Floor Leader; ranking member, Judiciary & Criminal Justice Subcommittee.

mike-gottlieb@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-102 (Davie/Sunrise, Broward), Democrat. TERM-LIMITED -- not running for reelection in 2026 (4 consecutive terms); HD-102 is an open seat. Conservative Freedom Index lifetime ~10%. FALSEs verified against official FL Legislature roll-call PDFs: NAY on HB 5 (2022 15-week ban) and SB 300 (2023 6-week ban); NAY on HB 1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay'); NAY on HB 1 (2023 vouchers); NAY on SB 1718 (2023 immigration/E-Verify). Mixed on guns: was NOT VOTING on HB 543 (2023 permitless carry) and voted NAY on HB 759 (2025) to keep the gun-age limit, BUT as a documented cross-party DEVIATION he sponsored HB 487 (2019) allowing tactical medical personnel to carry firearms alongside SWAT (signed into law) -- so self-defense cells are left null rather than scored as uniformly anti-gun. Reform-leaning on justice (opposed the 2023 non-unanimous-jury death-penalty expansion).

0.note: Voted NAY on both the 15-week ban (HB 5, 2022) and the 6-week ban (SB 300, 2023) -- pro-choice; does not affirm personhood from conception in law.

1.note: Voted against even restrictions -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022) 'Don't Say Gay' -- did not support restricting LGBTQ instruction in schools.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023), which restricts sanctuary policies and strengthens immigration enforcement.

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.
